description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel installation consisting of a single vertical metal pole supporting a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels. The solar panel array is composed of four individual modules arranged in a 2x2 grid, each with a dark, glassy surface typical of solar cells. The mounting structure appears to be made of metal, likely steel or aluminum, and is anchored into a patch of ground covered with grass or turf. The panel array is tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture, and the back of the panels shows a supporting frame with visible bolts and brackets. No electrical cabling or inverter housing is visible, suggesting these components may be concealed or located elsewhere. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for urban parks, sidewalks, or open community spaces.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a single metal pole, featuring four dark photovoltaic modules in a 2x2 grid, set above a grassy patch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the underside of the tilted solar panel array and the supporting pole centered on a grassy base.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the panel's tilt and the structural frame supporting the modules.
description_view_2: Direct front view displaying the full 2x2 grid of solar panels and the central mounting pole.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the panel's tilt and the mounting structure from the other side.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (likely south), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground-mounted with a metal (likely steel or aluminum) support structure
attribute_array_configuration: 2x2 grid of solar panels mounted together on a single pole
